description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a beige exterior, featuring two large, straight wings stacked one above the other and supported by a series of struts and wires. The wings display roundels with red, white, and blue concentric circles, suggesting military or historical insignia. The fuselage is slender and rectangular, with a tail section that includes a vertical stabilizer and a horizontal tailplane, both in matching beige. The airplane is equipped with two visible front-mounted propeller engines, each with exposed framework and red propeller hubs. The undercarriage consists of simple, fixed landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid. The cockpit is located near the front of the fuselage and appears to be open, with minimal canopy or window structure.
description: A beige, vintage biplane with dual stacked wings, front-mounted twin propellers, and military roundel markings. It features an open cockpit and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Oblique front view showing the twin front-mounted propellers, open cockpit, and detailed wing struts.
description_view_1: Rear oblique view highlighting the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the upper wing roundels.
description_view_2: Rear side view focusing on the tail section, wing structure, and the absence of visible propellers from this angle.
description_view_3: Oblique front view from the opposite side, clearly displaying both propellers, the open cockpit, and the wing struts.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane with straight, non-swept wings and prominent interplane struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Twin front-mounted piston engines with exposed propellers.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal tailplane.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit with multi-seat configuration and minimal canopy.
